metadata.scope_note: Nigeria v1 sector overlay pack. Use together with core_control_library for baseline privacy, cybersecurity, governance, evidence, and human-review controls.
metadata.versioning_note: This is a frozen starter snapshot. Do not mutate after use in reports; publish v2025_02 or later for changes.

official_sources_consulted.name: NITDA Code of Practice for Interactive Computer Service Platforms/Internet Intermediaries 2022
NITDA National Cloud Technical Document v1.03

limitations: Rules encode document-evidence checks, not a final legal determination.
Each rule requires validation against current Nigerian laws, regulations, regulator circulars, directives, codes and guidance before production reliance.

rules.title: Privacy-by-design and personal-data protection controls are documented
rules.summary: Technology-service documents should evidence privacy-by-design, data protection, lawful basis, consent, data-subject rights, or processor controls.

metadata.implementation_note: Evidence is text-derived and must be reviewed by a qualified compliance/legal reviewer before reliance.

rules.title: Cybersecurity and incident-response controls are present
rules.summary: Technology documents should evidence cybersecurity controls, incident response, vulnerability management, access control, or security monitoring.

metadata.implementation_note: Evidence is text-derived and must be reviewed by a qualified compliance/legal reviewer before reliance.

rules.title: Critical information infrastructure protection is addressed where applicable
rules.summary: Technology infrastructure documents should evidence CNII/critical information infrastructure assessment, protection plans, audit, or resilience controls where applicable.

metadata.implementation_note: Evidence is text-derived and must be reviewed by a qualified compliance/legal reviewer before reliance.

rules.title: Interactive platform / internet intermediary obligations are documented where applicable
rules.summary: Platform documents should evidence content moderation, user complaints, unlawful content handling, government agency contact, or platform transparency controls.

source_basis: NITDA Code of Practice for Interactive Computer Service Platforms/Internet Intermediaries 2022
metadata.implementation_note: Evidence is text-derived and must be reviewed by a qualified compliance/legal reviewer before reliance.

rules.title: Cloud-service compliance and data-location controls are documented where applicable
rules.summary: Cloud documents should evidence cloud-service listing/certification, data location, data residency, procurement controls, or technical standards.

metadata.implementation_note: Evidence is text-derived and must be reviewed by a qualified compliance/legal reviewer before reliance.

rules.title: NITDA approval, listing, clearance, or regulatory contact is referenced where applicable
rules.summary: Technology project documents should evidence NITDA approval, certification, project clearance, listing, or relevant regulatory contact where applicable.

metadata.implementation_note: Evidence is text-derived and must be reviewed by a qualified compliance/legal reviewer before reliance.

rules.title: User complaint, takedown, or redress process is documented
rules.summary: User-facing technology documents should evidence complaints, takedown, appeal, redress, support, or dispute handling.

source_basis: NITDA Code of Practice for Interactive Computer Service Platforms/Internet Intermediaries 2022
metadata.implementation_note: Evidence is text-derived and must be reviewed by a qualified compliance/legal reviewer before reliance.

rules.title: Corporate registration and local operating identity are present
rules.summary: Technology-service documents should reference corporate registration, Nigerian operating entity, RC number, or company registration.

source_basis: NITDA Code of Practice large-service-platform expectations
metadata.implementation_note: Evidence is text-derived and must be reviewed by a qualified compliance/legal reviewer before reliance.

rules.title: Cross-border transfer or third-party processing controls are documented
rules.summary: Technology documents should evidence safeguards for processors, subprocessors, cloud vendors, or cross-border transfers where applicable.

metadata.implementation_note: Evidence is text-derived and must be reviewed by a qualified compliance/legal reviewer before reliance.

rules.title: Backup, disaster recovery, and continuity controls are present
rules.summary: Technology operations documents should evidence backup, disaster recovery, business continuity, recovery objectives, or contingency controls.

metadata.implementation_note: Evidence is text-derived and must be reviewed by a qualified compliance/legal reviewer before reliance.
